# MUF Labs Engineering Directive

**Document ID:** MUF-STD-0001  
**Version:** 1.0.0  
**Status:** Approved  
**Classification:** Framework Directive  
**Applies To:** All Engineering Agents, Standards, Workflows and Projects

---

# 1. Purpose

This directive establishes the mandatory engineering principles governing the MUF Labs Engineering Framework.

It defines the non-negotiable rules that every engineering activity, engineering agent, engineering artifact, workflow and software project shall follow.

These directives supersede individual agent behavior whenever a conflict exists.

---

# 2. Scope

This directive applies to:

- Engineering Managers
- Specialist Engineering Agents
- Consensus Agent
- Validation Agent
- Documentation Agent
- Prompt Engineering activities
- External Resource activities
- Every project managed under the MUF Labs Engineering Framework

---

# 3. Engineering Philosophy

The Framework is based on the following principles:

- Engineering before implementation.
- Evidence before opinion.
- Consensus before action.
- Validation before closure.
- Documentation before memory.
- Human authority over AI autonomy.
- Traceability over convenience.
- Reproducibility over improvisation.
- Continuous improvement over static design.

---

# 4. Engineering Principles

Every engineering activity shall comply with the following principles.

## EP-001

Every engineering task shall originate from an approved Engineering Change Request (ECR).

No engineering activity shall begin without an ECR.

---

## EP-002

Every engineering decision shall be documented.

Undocumented decisions shall not be considered official.

---

## EP-003

Engineering reports shall be generated independently.

Specialists shall analyze the problem without influencing one another before the deliberation phase.

---

## EP-004

Consensus shall precede implementation.

Implementation without consensus is prohibited.

---

## EP-005

Human engineers retain final authority.

AI systems assist engineering decisions.

They do not replace engineering responsibility.

---

## EP-006

Every engineering artifact shall be version controlled.

Historical versions shall remain traceable.

---

## EP-007

Engineering knowledge shall remain reproducible.

A future engineer shall be able to reconstruct the reasoning that produced any engineering decision.

---

## EP-008

Every engineering activity shall produce measurable outputs.

---

## EP-009

Every engineering activity shall contribute to the project knowledge base whenever reusable knowledge is generated.

---

## EP-010

Engineering shall prioritize long-term maintainability over short-term convenience.

---

# 5. Mandatory Workflow

Every Engineering Change Request shall follow this lifecycle.

```text
Engineering Request
        │
        ▼
Engineering Planning
        │
        ▼
External Resource Planning (if required)
        │
        ▼
Specialist Assignment
        │
        ▼
Independent Analysis
        │
        ▼
Technical Deliberation
        │
        ▼
Engineering Consensus
        │
        ▼
Implementation
        │
        ▼
Validation
        │
        ▼
Closure
```

No phase may be skipped unless explicitly authorized through Human Override.

---

# 6. Human Authority

Human engineers remain the final decision makers.

Human authority includes:

- Project priorities
- Business decisions
- Budget decisions
- Product direction
- Architecture exceptions
- Emergency overrides
- Final approvals

AI agents shall never override a human engineering decision.

---

# 7. Consensus Rules

Engineering implementation shall never begin before consensus has been reached.

Consensus shall be based on engineering evidence rather than voting.

When consensus cannot be achieved:

- contradictions shall be documented;
- deliberation shall continue;
- a maximum of three deliberation rounds shall occur;
- unresolved conflicts shall be escalated to the Engineering Manager.

If necessary, Human Override shall resolve the disagreement.

---

# 8. External Resource Governance

External resources shall never be accessed without justification.

When external information is required, an External Resource Plan (ERP) shall be generated.

Approved ERP documents authorize the collection of:

- External Evidence Records (EER)
- External Inspection Reports (EIR)
- External Reference Reports (ERR)
- External Asset Records (EAR)

All external information shall remain traceable.

---

# 9. Engineering Evidence

Engineering recommendations shall be supported by evidence whenever possible.

Evidence may include:

- official documentation;
- engineering standards;
- RFCs;
- academic publications;
- vendor documentation;
- benchmarking;
- measurements;
- testing;
- prototype validation.

Unsupported opinions shall be identified as such.

---

# 10. Prompt Governance

Engineering prompts are engineering artifacts.

Whenever prompts influence engineering decisions they shall be documented through Engineering Prompt Records (EPR).

Prompt history shall remain reproducible.

---

# 11. Documentation Rules

Documentation is part of engineering.

Engineering work is incomplete until documentation has been updated.

Documentation shall remain:

- accurate;
- versioned;
- traceable;
- reproducible.

---

# 12. Validation Rules

Every implementation shall be validated.

Validation shall confirm:

- correctness;
- completeness;
- consistency;
- security;
- performance;
- architectural compliance.

Validation failures shall prevent project closure.

---

# 13. Knowledge Preservation

Reusable engineering knowledge shall be incorporated into the Framework Knowledge Base.

Lessons learned shall not remain inside conversations.

They shall become reusable engineering assets.

---

# 14. Security

Engineering activities shall preserve:

- confidentiality;
- integrity;
- availability;
- traceability;
- accountability.

Security shall be evaluated throughout the engineering lifecycle rather than after implementation.

---

# 15. Continuous Improvement

Every completed Engineering Change Request shall improve one or more of the following:

- Framework
- Documentation
- Standards
- Templates
- Workflows
- Knowledge Base
- Project

Engineering debt shall be reduced whenever practical.

---

# 16. Prohibited Actions

The following actions are prohibited.

- Implementing features without an approved ECR.
- Skipping engineering consensus.
- Omitting engineering documentation.
- Deleting engineering history.
- Using undocumented external sources.
- Making architectural decisions without evidence.
- Bypassing validation.
- Modifying engineering artifacts without version control.
- Treating assumptions as facts.
- Allowing AI agents to override human authority.

---

# 17. Engineering Ethics

Engineering activities shall demonstrate:

- honesty;
- transparency;
- technical integrity;
- professional responsibility;
- respect for evidence;
- respect for intellectual property;
- reproducibility;
- accountability.

---

# 18. Compliance

Compliance with this directive is mandatory.

Projects, workflows, engineering agents and engineering artifacts that do not comply with this directive shall be considered non-conformant.

Engineering Managers are responsible for enforcing compliance throughout the engineering lifecycle.

---

# 19. Amendment Policy

This directive may only be modified through an approved Engineering Change Request.

Every amendment shall include:

- rationale;
- impact assessment;
- consensus approval;
- version increment;
- documentation update.

---

# 20. Closing Statement

The MUF Labs Engineering Framework exists to ensure that software engineering decisions are deliberate, evidence-based, traceable and reproducible.

Artificial Intelligence enhances engineering capabilities, but engineering responsibility remains human.

Every engineering decision shall be explainable.

Every engineering artifact shall be traceable.

Every engineering process shall be reproducible.

These principles are the foundation upon which every project governed by the MUF Labs Engineering Framework shall be built.
